title: Tragacantha sivasica

intro: Tragacantha sivasica, also known as Sivas knapweed, is a plant species within the family Asteraceae, native to Turkey. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in relieving pain and reducing inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Tragacantha sivasica include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
